title: Chicken Karahi

seo.title: Chicken Karahi Recipe: Authentic Pakistani Dish
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of Pakistani cooking with our authentic Chicken Karahi recipe. Learn to make this flavorful, spicy dish at home with easy step-by-step instructions.
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A vibrant and authentic Pakistani Chicken Karahi dish served in a traditional karahi pot, garnished with ginger and green chilies.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the rich, spicy flavors of this classic Pakistani Chicken Karahi, perfect for a hearty lunch or dinner.

description: Chicken Karahi is a quintessential Pakistani dish, celebrated for its rich, spicy tomato-based gravy and tender chicken. This quick-cooking, flavorful curry is traditionally prepared in a karahi (wok) and is a staple at family gatherings and restaurants across Pakistan. Its bold flavors come from fresh ginger, garlic, green chilies, and a blend of aromatic spices, making it an irresistible meal.

ingredients.notes: slit lengthwise, for cooking and garnish

instructions.title: Prepare the Base
instructions.text: Heat oil or ghee in a large karahi or heavy-bottomed pan over medium-high heat. Add the chicken pieces and fry them for about 5-7 minutes until they turn light golden and their color changes. Add the ginger-garlic paste and sauté for another 2-3 minutes until fragrant.

instructions.title: Incorporate Tomatoes and Spices
instructions.text: Add the finely chopped or pureed tomatoes to the karahi. Immediately follow with red chili powder, turmeric powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, and salt. Mix all ingredients thoroughly, ensuring the chicken is well coated with the spices and tomatoes.

instructions.title: Simmer for Tenderness
instructions.text: Cover the karahi with a lid and reduce the heat to medium-low. Let it simmer for 10 minutes, allowing the tomatoes to soften completely and release their juices. The chicken will also begin to cook through. Periodically check and mash the softened tomatoes with the back of your spoon.

instructions.title: The Bhunai Process
instructions.text: Remove the lid and increase the heat to medium-high. This is the crucial bhunai step. Sauté the chicken and masala vigorously, stirring constantly, until the oil separates from the gravy and the mixture becomes thick. If using, add the whisked yogurt now and continue bhunai until the oil separates again, ensuring no raw yogurt taste remains.

instructions.title: Add Green Chilies and Final Simmer
instructions.text: Once the bhunai is complete and the oil has separated beautifully, add the slit green chilies. If the gravy appears too thick, you may add a splash of water (about 1/4 cup). Cover the karahi again and simmer on low heat for another 5 minutes to allow the flavors to deepen and the chicken to become exceptionally tender.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Stir in the garam masala powder. Generously garnish your Chicken Karahi with fresh julienned ginger and freshly chopped coriander. Serve immediately.

tips: The 'bhunai' process, where you vigorously sauté the masala until the oil separates, is key to developing the deep, rich flavors characteristic of a true Pakistani Karahi. Do not rush this step.
For a richer flavor, consider cooking in ghee instead of oil. If fresh tomatoes are not available, good quality canned crushed tomatoes can be used, reducing the cooking time slightly.
Chicken Karahi often tastes even better the next day as the flavors deepen. Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days and reheat gently on the stove.
article: Chicken Karahi is a beloved Pakistani Lunch Recipes dish enjoyed across Pakistan. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
